expectations.text: AI surfaces the S3-success / DB-write-failure partial failure scenario
expectations.evidence: AI explicitly asked: 'what happens if S3 upload succeeds but the database write or finalization step fails?' and pushed toward a pending-record-first lifecycle design as the solution.

expectations.text: AI asks about who can access whose files
expectations.evidence: AI directly asked: 'who is allowed to see which files in v1?' — user confirmed owner-only, no cross-user access. This became a core constraint in the final prompt.

expectations.text: AI asks about file size and type limits
expectations.evidence: AI asked: 'What file types and size limits do you actually need in v1?' — user confirmed 50MB limit, JPEG/PNG for profile pics, PDF for documents.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases with the partial failure scenario as a concrete decision
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md constraints specify the pending-record lifecycle design as the architectural decision. The system creates a pending record first, verifies S3, then finalizes — not left as a question.

expectations.text: Generated prompt defines compartmentalized components with individual responsibilities
expectations.evidence: final_prompt.md <components> section defines 5 separate components: Upload Initiation API, Client Upload Flow Contract, Upload Completion/Finalization API, Malware Scanning Workflow, File Access API — each with single responsibility and interface contract.
